arts.ttl: Brendon Aaronson 'energy' message as Leeds United transfer decision in spotlight
arts.sum: Daniel Farke says he is not concerned about Brenden Aaronson becoming fatigued this season, with Leeds United currently possessing no direct back-up at number 10. Farke outlined towards the end of the window that he only wanted a recruit from the winter market if he was of good enough quality, was of great character and affordable.

arts.ttl: Leeds United loan star answers future question as summer transfer decision looms
arts.sum: The 30-year-old joined Leeds United on a season-long loan deal from Bournemouth back in the summer. By joining the Leeds United community on WhatsApp, you'll see the biggest and best stories as soon as they happen.

arts.ttl: Key defender selection call, Leeds mindset, Frank Lampard revenge vs Coventry
arts.sum: It is easy to get elated after such a thrashing of a league rival, but Farke said post-match that his players should enjoy the annihilation of Cardiff albeit keep a level head. Frank Lampard has strung together a series of results at the CBS Arena and will feel confident of causing Leeds frustration - and not for the first time.

arts.ttl: Leeds United man discusses chances of PL club transfer agreement
arts.sum: Leeds United midfielder Joe Rothwell has been an incredibly shrewd addition to Daniel Farke’s side this season on loan, but there are doubts over whether he will be a long-term investment. However, it’s entirely fair to say that is the case with Leeds signing Joe Rothwell on loan and then Ao Tanaka for a bargain fee.

arts.ttl: ‘It’s been tough’ – Midfielder makes admission over Leeds United loan despite on-pitch success
arts.sum: Leeds United loan star Joe Rothwell is pleased with how his Elland Road spell has gone but admits… The post ‘It’s been tough’ – Midfielder makes admission over Leeds United loan despite on-pitch success appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Leeds United predicted XI vs Coventry City as Junior Firpo starts and Pascal Struijk call made
arts.sum: Ethan Ampadu has played solidly alongside Rodon and will likely continue there should Struijk be unable to start. Get Leeds United news to your phone with our WhatsApp community

arts.ttl: Leeds United squad revealed for Coventry City as Daniel Farke drops big selection hint
arts.sum: Leeds United squad vs Coventry City: Meslier, Darlow, Cairns, Bogle, Rodon, Struijk, Debayo, Schmidt, Wober, Firpo, James, Gnonto, Gruev, Ampadu, Rothwell, Tanaka, Guilavogui, Solomon, Ramazani, Aaronson, Joseph, Piroe. Get Leeds United news to your phone with our WhatsApp community

arts.ttl: Leeds United suffer Premier League promotion blow after Burnley clash
arts.sum: Leeds United have suffered a blow in their hopes of gaining promotion to the Premier League after Burnley picked up a vital three points against Oxford United. Unsurprisingly, Burnley started the match on the front foot but were frustrating their home supporters by failing to capitalise on their chances.

arts.ttl: Daniel Farke gives bold Leeds United transfer verdict after Southampton setback
arts.sum: Daniel Farke is confident in his Leeds squad for promotion this season, and you can’t exactly blame him as they sit top with the best goals record and second-best defence thus far. If we’re really honest, does a player who doesn’t make it into the top 17-18 players in a bottom half PL squad, does he really improve Leeds United?

arts.ttl: Lampard’s Coventry revival: from last-chance saloon to promotion charge
arts.sum: Manager has silenced doubters by leading a resurgent Sky Blues side with the most productive midfield in the division “Super Frankie Lampard.” One only had to listen to the chant by Coventry’s away supporters saluting their new-ish manager on Saturday after the Sky Blues’ fourth-straight league victory – an utterly dominant 2-0 away win at Swansea – to see the transformational effect the Englishman has had in his 14 games in charge of the Championship club. Coventry were just above the relegation zone when Mark Robins was sacked in November. Despite City’s poor start to the season, there were doubts among supporters (and even the odd journalist) at the appointment of Lampard and the sacking of Robins, who had previously been approaching legendary status (and later given an honorary doctorate by Coventry University for his impact on the club and the city).
